title: URP Material & Shader Workflow
summary: End-to-end workflow for creating and configuring materials on the Universal Render Pipeline: which URP shader to pick (Lit, Simple Lit, Unlit, Particles Lit, Complex Lit), how to drive surface mode / blending / alpha clipping through float properties (_Surface, _Blend, _AlphaClip, _Cutoff), and why feature toggles like emission, normal maps, and metallic maps require BOTH a property write AND a matching shader keyword (_EMISSION, _NORMALMAP, _METALLICSPECGLOSSMAP). Use before any mosaic material/create or set-property call targeting URP — it prevents the four most common failure modes: pink materials, invisible transparency, dark emission, flat normals.

llmGuidance.when_creating_any_urp_material: Default to shaderName 'Universal Render Pipeline/Lit' unless a specific reason exists (Unlit for FX/UI, Simple Lit for mobile, Particles Lit for VFX). Never use 'Standard' on URP — it renders pink.
llmGuidance.when_making_a_material_transparent: You MUST do all four: set _Surface=1 (Transparent), set _Blend=0 (Alpha, or 1/2/3 for other modes), set material.renderQueue to 3000 (Transparent), and EnableKeyword('_SURFACE_TYPE_TRANSPARENT'). Setting only one of the four produces a silent visual bug.
llmGuidance.when_enabling_emission: Three steps. 1) material.SetColor('_EmissionColor', hdrColor) — values >1 for bloom. 2) material.EnableKeyword('_EMISSION'). 3) optionally GlobalIllumination.EmissiveIsBlack=false via material.globalIlluminationFlags. Skipping step 2 leaves the material dark even though the property reads back correctly.
llmGuidance.when_assigning_a_normal_map: material.SetTexture('_BumpMap', tex) + material.EnableKeyword('_NORMALMAP'). The texture's import settings must be Texture Type=Normal map. Without the keyword the sampler runs but the shader branch is dead-code, so the map has no visible effect.
llmGuidance.when_choosing_alphaClip_vs_transparent: Alpha clip (_AlphaClip=1 + keyword _ALPHATEST_ON) for foliage, chain-link fences, cutouts — casts shadows, batches with opaque, sorts per-pixel. Transparent for glass, smoke, glow — breaks opaque sorting, is more expensive, order-dependent. Prefer alpha clip when the silhouette is what matters.
llmGuidance.when_Shader_Find_returns_null_for_a_URP_: Pipeline shaders are stripped from builds unless referenced. Add the shader to Project Settings > Graphics > Always Included Shaders, or keep a material asset in the scene that uses it. Shader.Find only works in the editor reliably.
llmGuidance.when_creating_material_at_runtime: Prefer holding a serialized Material reference on a MonoBehaviour and calling new Material(reference). This avoids Shader.Find entirely and survives build stripping. Shader.Find should be considered a last resort.
llmGuidance.when_setting_color_property: URP Lit uses _BaseColor, not _Color. _Color still exists as a hidden backward-compat alias and accepts writes silently without any visible effect. See kb-rendering-shader-properties for the full cross-pipeline name table.

shaderSelection.summary: URP ships five first-party shaders covering the common lighting models. Pick by use-case; do not default to Lit for everything — unlit FX and particles have better-suited shaders that also cost less.

shaders.use: General purpose opaque/transparent surfaces. Default choice.
Mobile, stylized art, lower-end hardware. Cheaper than Lit.
UI overlays, holograms, procedural effects, skyboxes authored as materials.
Particle System renderers that need lighting (smoke, dust, embers with PBR).
Car paint, lacquered surfaces, layered materials. URP 12+ only. More expensive.

shaderSelection.antiPattern: Assigning the Built-in 'Standard' shader to a URP material yields the pink error shader because Standard is not valid under URP. Always prefix URP shader paths with 'Universal Render Pipeline/'.

propertyReference.summary: Full per-shader property tables live in kb-rendering-shader-properties — do not duplicate. URP Lit's most-used properties, at a glance:

keyProperties.notes: PBR workflow only. Ignored in Specular workflow.

propertyReference.hdrpDifference: HDRP Lit uses _BaseColorMap (not _BaseMap), _NormalMap (not _BumpMap), and _EmissiveColor (not _EmissionColor). If Mosaic generates cross-pipeline code, branch by pipeline — see kb-rendering-pipeline-detection.

surfaceModes.summary: URP Lit/Unlit expose a dual-float surface model. _Surface chooses opaque vs transparent; _Blend chooses the blend math when transparent. Changing these at runtime requires also rewriting the render queue and the matching keyword — Unity's Editor inspector does all of this in one click, scripting does not.
surfaceModes.runtimeRecipe: To flip an opaque material to transparent at runtime: mat.SetFloat('_Surface', 1); mat.SetFloat('_Blend', 0); mat.SetFloat('_ZWrite', 0); mat.renderQueue = 3000; mat.EnableKeyword('_SURFACE_TYPE_TRANSPARENT'); mat.DisableKeyword('_ALPHATEST_ON'); mat.SetOverrideTag('RenderType','Transparent'); mat.SetInt('_SrcBlend',(int)BlendMode.SrcAlpha); mat.SetInt('_DstBlend',(int)BlendMode.OneMinusSrcAlpha);

alphaAndCutout.summary: Alpha clipping and transparency solve different problems and have very different performance envelopes. Pick one; do not enable both.

alphaClip.use: Foliage, fences, hair cards, decals with hard edges.
alphaClip.pros: Casts shadows. Batches with opaque. No sort order issues. Cheap.
alphaClip.cons: Hard edges only (no partial transparency). GPU early-Z suffers slightly.

transparent.use: Glass, water, smoke, glow, UI with fading alpha.
transparent.pros: Smooth alpha gradient. Custom blend modes.
transparent.cons: Per-object sort order. Overdraw cost. Shadow casting is limited/approximate. Defeats SRP Batcher grouping with opaque.

emission.summary: Emission is the textbook example of URP's keyword-gated feature pattern. Setting the color alone does nothing. The shader has a compile-time branch guarded by _EMISSION; if the keyword is off, the emission code is dead.
steps: mat.SetColor('_EmissionColor', color); — color can exceed 1 in each channel for HDR intensity that bloom will pick up.
mat.EnableKeyword('_EMISSION'); — without this, the color is written but never sampled.
mat.SetTexture('_EmissionMap', tex); — optional, multiplies with the color.
mat.globalIlluminationFlags = MaterialGlobalIlluminationFlags.BakedEmissive; — optional, makes emission contribute to baked lightmaps.
emission.bloom: HDR emission values only produce a glow when a Volume with a Bloom override is present in the scene AND the URP asset's post-processing is enabled. Without bloom, intensity 5 looks identical to intensity 1 on screen.

normalMaps.summary: Two independent failure points: (1) the texture must be imported as Normal map, (2) the shader keyword must be enabled. Either omission produces a flat-looking surface with no error.
steps: In the importer: Texture Type=Normal map. Unity swaps the channel layout; without this, samples read from the wrong channels.
mat.EnableKeyword('_NORMALMAP'); — gates the tangent-space fetch in URP Lit's fragment shader.
normalMaps.commonFailure: Script sets _BumpMap but forgets _NORMALMAP — the texture is bound to a sampler that is never read. Material inspector shows the map but the surface stays smooth.

keywordSystem.summary: URP shaders use compile-time multi_compile / shader_feature keywords to keep variants cheap. Setting a property sets data; enabling a keyword selects which code path runs. Most URP features require both.
keywordSystem.stripping: Unity strips unused keyword variants from builds. If a runtime EnableKeyword call references a variant that was never seen during asset authoring, the keyword is stripped and the call is a no-op in the built player. Add materials that exercise each needed variant to the build, or register the shader in Graphics Settings > Shader Stripping.

materialInstancing.summary: Two overlapping concepts that are easy to conflate. GPU instancing batches many draws of one material into one draw call. MaterialPropertyBlock lets you vary a property per renderer without cloning the material.

gpuInstancing.setup: mat.enableInstancing = true; — this is the primary toggle. Some custom shaders additionally require EnableKeyword('INSTANCING_ON'), but URP's built-in shaders handle this internally.
gpuInstancing.use: Many instances of the same mesh+material (rocks, grass, foliage cards).

materialPropertyBlock.setup: var block = new MaterialPropertyBlock(); block.SetColor('_BaseColor', c); renderer.SetPropertyBlock(block);
materialPropertyBlock.use: Per-renderer color/float/texture variations without breaking instancing or creating N material instances.
materialPropertyBlock.caveat: MaterialPropertyBlock cannot toggle keywords. For per-instance emission ON/OFF you still need separate materials or a shader that branches on a vector property.

materialInstancing.instancePerRenderer: Accessing Renderer.material (not sharedMaterial) clones the material, giving each renderer its own copy. Cheap for a handful of objects, disastrous at scale — breaks instancing and bloats memory.

runtimeMaterialCreation.summary: Four ways to get a Material at runtime, in descending order of preference.

options.method: Serialized Material reference on a MonoBehaviour
options.pros: Works in builds, no strings, survives stripping.

options.cons: Needs folder conventions; Resources inflates build size.

options.pros: Clone a configured template without touching shaders.
options.cons: Each clone is a separate asset at runtime.
Shader.Find returns null in builds if shader is stripped. Treat as last resort.

runtimeMaterialCreation.rule: In Mosaic-generated editor scripts, Shader.Find is acceptable because the editor holds every shader. In Mosaic-generated runtime/player code, prefer a serialized reference or Resources.Load.

urpVersionMatrix.summary: Property names and keyword conventions have been stable across URP majors 10-17. Breaking changes are rare; most deltas are additive shader features.

versions.note: Older LTS. Same property names as current. Complex Lit not present.
Complex Lit introduced. Render Graph not yet default.
Forward+ renderer path added. No Lit property renames.
Render Graph default. Same Lit property names. Stable.

commonFailureModes.cause: Shader not found or wrong pipeline (Standard on URP).
commonFailureModes.fix: Set shader to 'Universal Render Pipeline/Lit'. Verify pipeline via kb-rendering-pipeline-detection.

commonFailureModes.cause: _Surface set to 1 but render queue still 2000, or keyword missing.

commonFailureModes.symptom: Emission color set but material stays dark
Normal map assigned but surface stays flat
commonFailureModes.cause: _NORMALMAP keyword missing, or texture not imported as Normal map.
commonFailureModes.fix: Enable _NORMALMAP keyword and set TextureImporter.textureType = Normal.

commonFailureModes.symptom: Color change has no effect on URP material
commonFailureModes.cause: Used _Color (Built-in name) on URP Lit. Writes to hidden alias slot.

commonFailureModes.symptom: Shader.Find returns null in build only
commonFailureModes.cause: Shader stripped from build because unreferenced.
commonFailureModes.fix: Add to Graphics > Always Included Shaders, or reference a material asset in a scene.

decisionRules.scenario: Mosaic tool needs to create an opaque colored URP material
decisionRules.rule: 1) Assert pipeline is URP. 2) new Material with Shader.Find('Universal Render Pipeline/Lit') or a referenced shader. 3) SetColor('_BaseColor', color). Done — default _BaseMap is white, no keyword needed.
decisionRules.evidence: URP Lit.shader source declares _BaseColor with [MainColor] and _BaseMap default white.

decisionRules.scenario: Mosaic tool needs to create a transparent URP material (glass, water surface)
decisionRules.rule: Apply full.surfaceModes.runtimeRecipe exactly. All six settings (_Surface, _Blend, _ZWrite, renderQueue, keyword, blend modes) are mandatory — no partial states render correctly.
decisionRules.evidence: URP Lit.shader surface.hlsl branches on _SURFACE_TYPE_TRANSPARENT keyword; blend state is read from _SrcBlend/_DstBlend material ints.

decisionRules.scenario: Mosaic tool needs a glowing material
decisionRules.rule: SetColor('_EmissionColor', hdrColor) then EnableKeyword('_EMISSION'). For glow-on-screen also require a URP Volume with Bloom in the scene. Intensity > 1 requires HDR color.
decisionRules.evidence: URP Lit fragment shader #ifdef _EMISSION guards EmissionColor sampling.

decisionRules.scenario: Mosaic tool needs per-instance color on many renderers
decisionRules.rule: Use MaterialPropertyBlock + renderer.SetPropertyBlock. Do NOT assign renderer.material per instance — it clones and breaks batching.
decisionRules.evidence: Unity SRP Batcher docs: MaterialPropertyBlock variants are batcher-compatible; per-instance Material clones are not.

decisionRules.rule: Shader Lit or Simple Lit, _AlphaClip=1, _Cutoff=0.5, EnableKeyword('_ALPHATEST_ON'), renderQueue=2450. Do NOT also set _Surface=1 — that's transparency, not cutout.
decisionRules.evidence: URP Lit.shader AlphaClip.hlsl gated by _ALPHATEST_ON keyword; alpha-test queue is 2450 per Unity standard.

antiPatterns.mistake: Using Shader.Find('Standard') on a URP project
antiPatterns.why: Standard is the Built-in PBR shader. URP does not render it, so the material falls back to pink error shader.
antiPatterns.instead: Use 'Universal Render Pipeline/Lit'. Detect pipeline first — see kb-rendering-pipeline-detection.

antiPatterns.mistake: Setting _Color on a URP Lit material
antiPatterns.why: URP Lit exposes _Color as a hidden backward-compat alias. The write succeeds silently but the value is never sampled.
antiPatterns.instead: Use _BaseColor. Guard cross-pipeline code with Material.HasProperty.

antiPatterns.why: URP Lit's emission branch is #ifdef _EMISSION. Without the keyword the color is inert.
antiPatterns.instead: Always pair SetColor('_EmissionColor', c) with EnableKeyword('_EMISSION').

antiPatterns.mistake: Flipping _Surface to 1 and assuming transparency works
antiPatterns.why: _Surface by itself only changes a uniform. Render queue stays at Opaque (2000), blend state is wrong, and the transparent keyword is off, so the object renders opaque or not at all.
antiPatterns.instead: Apply the full six-step recipe in full.surfaceModes.runtimeRecipe.

antiPatterns.mistake: Using renderer.material in a loop to tint many objects
antiPatterns.why: Each access clones the material, breaks SRP Batcher, and allocates per-object memory.
antiPatterns.instead: MaterialPropertyBlock. One shared material, per-renderer property overrides.

antiPatterns.mistake: Relying on Shader.Find in built player code
antiPatterns.why: Unused shaders are stripped from builds. Shader.Find returns null and the code path silently creates a null-shader material.
antiPatterns.instead: Hold a serialized Material or Shader reference, or add to Always Included Shaders.

antiPatterns.mistake: Assigning a normal map without changing the texture's import type
antiPatterns.why: Color-type textures have swizzled channels relative to normal maps. The sampler runs, but the decoded normal is garbage.
antiPatterns.instead: TextureImporter.textureType = TextureImporterType.NormalMap. Reimport. Then set _BumpMap and enable _NORMALMAP.

versionDeltas.change: LWRP renamed to URP. Shader path prefix changed from 'Lightweight Render Pipeline/' to 'Universal Render Pipeline/'. Old names retained as aliases for one release cycle then removed.
versionDeltas.migrationNote: All Shader.Find strings must be updated. Mosaic targets 2021.3+ so this delta is informational for legacy-project detection only.

versionDeltas.change: Complex Lit shader added for layered surfaces (clear coat). Existing Lit/Simple Lit/Unlit property names unchanged.
Forward+ renderer path added. ShaderLab SHADERPASS_FORWARD variants expanded. No user-facing property renames on first-party shaders.
Render Graph becomes default render path. First-party Lit/Unlit/Simple Lit property names and keyword conventions preserved for compatibility.
versionDeltas.migrationNote: Custom renderer features written against the legacy ScriptableRenderPass API may need porting, but material-level code is unaffected.

authorshipNotes: Content authored by Mosaic team from publicly-documented Unity APIs, URP Manual pages, and open-source URP shader source (Unity-Technologies/Graphics, Unity Companion License). Property names, keyword identifiers, render queue constants, and API signatures are factual (not copyrightable). All explanatory prose, decision rules, runtime recipes, anti-patterns, failure-mode tables, and llmGuidance are original Mosaic editorial content. Unity Manual and URP package docs are cited in externalReferences and NOT reproduced here.
